Japanese: 同日　某時刻 おぼろ橋
English: February 8 Dusky Bridge

Japanese: （ハナシに聞いていたとおり、 かなり冷えるな）
English: (It sure is cold alright...)

Japanese: （これが‥‥ あの《おぼろ橋》か‥‥）
English: (So this is it... Dusky Bridge...)

Japanese: うごほおッ！ み、御剣検事どのォッ！
English: Ahem! M-Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: む‥‥イトノコギリ刑事。
English: Urk... Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: おひさしぶりッス！　１年ぶり ‥‥いや、もっとッスっけ？
English: Long time no see! It's been about a year? ...Or has it been longer?

Japanese: どうでもいいが、こんなところで 何をほっつき歩いている？
English: It doesn't matter, Detective. What does matter is why you're shuffling around up here.

Japanese: うごほほおッ！ ‥‥いきなり、ガツンと来たァ！
English: Oh, ouch! ...And there was a sharp left jab!

Japanese: でも、うれしいッス！ それでこそ、御剣検事ッス！
English: Well, I'm happy to see you anyway, Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: ‥‥もしかして、 人事異動でもあったのか？
English: Let me guess... You were transferred into another section at work.

Japanese: ムサいキミにピッタリの、 サムいところだが。
English: A good choice. The vast amount of nothing up here must be quite easy to guard.

Japanese: 御剣検事が帰国されると聞いて、 ムリヤリ出張してきたッス！
English: I heard you were back in the country and arranged to come all the way out here!

Japanese: みんな親切だから、 捜査にまぜてもらってるッス。
English: Everybody was real nice. They even let me take charge of the investigation, sir.

Japanese: （‥‥あいかわらず、 ムチャなオトコだな‥‥）
English: (...Gumshoe indeed! Like gum on your shoe, he's impossible to get rid of!)

Japanese: 事件のことは、 現場で詳しく報告するッス！
English: I'm supposed to report on the details of the crime scene, sir!

Japanese: 今日はこの糸鋸　圭介、 トコトンお供するッス！
English: Anyway! Here I am! Detective Dick Gumshoe! Reporting for duty!

Japanese: ‥‥そうか。ごくろう。
English: ...Great. Um, thank you, Detective.

Japanese: まったく‥‥ゴドー検事が来る ハズだったッスのに。
English: I thought Prosecutor Godot was gonna get here before me.

Japanese: ホント、困ったヒトッス！
English: That guy's a real mystery, I tell you!

Japanese: （ゴドー‥‥検事‥‥？）
English: (...Prosecutor Godot?)

Japanese: 野性味あふれる石コロに、 鬼気せまる文字がおどっている。
English: Some letters are wildly engraved into a roughly cut boulder.

Japanese: 《おぼろ橋》‥‥か。 ふさわしい名前のようだな。
English: "Dusky Bridge"... It certainly is an appropriate name.

Japanese: いやいや、御剣検事。 よく読まなきゃダメッス。
English: You need to get some glasses, Mr. Edgeworth! That sign says, "Dusty Bridge".

Japanese: まちがえやすいッスけど、 《おんぼろ橋》ッス。
English: I can see how you read it wrong, though, sir.

Japanese: ‥‥‥キミこそ、もう一度 よく読んでみたまえ。
English: You're the one who needs glasses, Detective. Try reading it one more time.

Japanese: ああああッ！　よく読んだら 《おぼろ橋》だったッス！
English: Ah! You're right! It's "Dusky Bridge" after all!

Japanese: きっと、書きまちがえたッスね。
English: I guess whoever wrote this made a mistake!

Japanese: あっ、公衆電話ッス！ 今どき、めずらしいッス！
English: Hey! It's a public phone! You don't see a lot of these anymore!

Japanese: ‥‥そうだな。
English: ...That's true.

Japanese: 御剣検事！　せっかくだから 記念写真を撮るッス！
English: Since we've got one here, why don't we take a photo as a memento!

Japanese: ‥‥ま、まあ、かまわないが。
English: Well, um, sure. Why not?

Japanese: ほい、しまった！ カメラがなかったッス！
English: Oh, darn it! I don't have a camera with me!

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと、ひとっ走り 使い捨てカメラを買ってくるッス！
English: ...I'm gonna go buy a disposable camera! I'll be right back, sir!

Japanese: （公衆電話の何が、そこまで 刑事をひきつけるのだろう‥‥）
English: (What's so special about public phones, and why is he so fascinated by them...?)

Japanese: コレをわたろうとしたのか。 ‥‥成歩堂のヤツ。
English: So this is the bridge Wright tried to cross?

Japanese: とんだお調子者ッスね。
English: Pretty reckless, if you ask me.

Japanese: それにしても、ここから 落ちて、ブジだったとは‥‥
English: I'm amazed he survived the fall from up here...

Japanese: ビックリ人間ッスね。
English: Yeah, he's one lucky guy, sir.

Japanese: いつも、法廷でブジな理由が わかったような気がするッス。
English: Now I see how he manages to win his cases in court. Blind luck.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥）
English: (I think dumb luck suits Wright just a bit better.)

Japanese: この先には、山小屋があるッス。 《極楽庵》という名前の。
English: There's a little shack down that way called, "Heavenly Hall".

Japanese: 山小屋‥‥？
English: A shack...?

Japanese: 冷たい風の吹きおろす、 負け犬のハキダメみたいな‥‥
English: It's like a run-down doghouse for losers that can't bear the freezing drafts of wind...

Japanese: なんとなく、自分の アパートを思い出すッス。
English: Kinda reminds me of my apartment, sir.

Japanese: 《極楽庵》と聞くと、 リッパなイメージだが‥‥
English: The name "Heavenly Hall" makes it sound like a palace...

Japanese: ボロ小屋にリッパな名前を つけるのは、ある種、犯罪ッス！
English: Giving a hovel a great name is a crime itself! I'd call it false advertising!

Japanese: ちなみに、自分のアパートの名前は 《メゾン・ド・あした》ッス。
English: By the way, the name of my apartment complex is "Compton Castles"...

Japanese: ‥‥それは、“リッパ”とは ちょっとちがうようだが。
English: ...That's not such a great name, if you ask me.

Japanese: ‥‥アパートとも ちょっとちがうッス。
English: Well, it's not such a great apartment either, sir.

Japanese: 向こう岸まで、ずいぶん キョリがあるようだな。
English: It looks like a pretty good distance to that far cliff.

Japanese: そッスねー‥‥ １００メートルぐらいッスか。
English: Yeah... It's gotta be around 100 yards or so.

Japanese: いくらなんでも それはないだろう、刑事。
English: Detective... That's not even CLOSE to a hundred yards.

Japanese: いやー。自分は、ものごとを 立体的に見るのが苦手ッスから。
English: Well... I gotta admit I'm not very good at judging distances.

Japanese: （２０メートルていど、か。 わたるのは不可能だな‥‥）
English: (It's about 20 yards or so. And impossible to cross without a bridge, it seems...)

Japanese: とにかく‥‥帰国したばかりで、 何もわからないのだが‥‥。
English: I just got back into the country, so I don't really know much about the case...

Japanese: 事件は、単純ッス！
English: It's simple!

Japanese: ‥‥単純なキミに断言されると いささか、サムさがつのるな。
English: Well, simple is as simple does, as they say.

Japanese: くううう‥‥刺すようなヒニク。 もお、なつかしいッス！
English: Oh, you've got no idea how much I've missed that biting sarcasm of yours, sir!

Japanese: ‥‥でも、今回は安心ッス。
English: ...But seriously, this one's a piece of cake.

Japanese: 決定的な目撃証人がいるッスから。
English: There's a witness that saw the whole thing!

Japanese: 目撃証人‥‥？
English: A witness...?

Japanese: もちろん、ビキニのおばさんッス。
English: Yeah, that Bikini lady.

Japanese: び、ビキニ‥‥？ このサムい山奥でか？
English: "Bikini lady"? Here? On this freezing cold mountain?

Japanese: ‥‥詳しくは、御剣検事が会って ハナシを聞いてほしいッス。
English: ...Well, you should talk to her yourself if you want the details, sir.

Japanese: （“決定的”というのと、 “ビキニ”が気になるな‥‥）
English: (I may have to talk to this "bikini lady"... I mean, "decisive witness" myself...)

Japanese: ゴドー検事、というのは‥‥？ 聞いたことのない名前だが。
English: So who is this Prosecutor Godot? I've never heard of him.

Japanese: 御剣検事がこの国を去ってから 出てきた、ヒヨッ子ッス！
English: Yeah, he's a new guy... Showed up after you left the country!

Japanese: ズブの新人ッスが‥‥なぜか、 ダレもアタマが上がらないッス。
English: He's a complete rookie, but nobody can say a bad word about the guy.

Japanese: どんなオトコだ？
English: What kind of a man is he?

Japanese: 検事になったのは最近ッスが‥‥ かなりのヤリ手らしいッス。
English: He just became a prosecutor recently, but he's good, sir. Real good.

Japanese: （そんなオトコなら、私の耳に 入っているハズなのだが‥‥）
English: (If he's so good, how is it that I've never heard of him...?)

Japanese: そいつが、この事件の 担当検事なのか？
English: Is he the lead prosecutor on this case?

Japanese: そッス！　なんといっても、 アイツがからんでいるッスから。
English: You bet he is! After all, "you know who" is right in the middle of it.
"You know who"?

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一ッス、モチロン。
English: Phoenix Wright, of course!

Japanese: ゴドー検事‥‥なぜか、あの 弁護士をつけ回しているッス。
English: For some reason, Godot has really got it in for Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 成歩堂を‥‥
English: Oh?

Japanese: ナニか、恨みでも あるんじゃないッスかねえ。
English: Yeah, he seems to have some kind of a grudge.

Japanese: “恨み”というと‥‥？
English: And what would be the cause of this grudge...?

Japanese: たとえば‥‥ あの仮面を笑われた、とか。
English: I dunno... Maybe he made fun of his mask or something.

Japanese: （‥‥自分で調べるとするか）
English: (None of this is making any sense. I'd better look into this Godot myself.)

Japanese: ところで‥‥これが、 成歩堂が踏みコワしたという橋か。
English: So this is the bridge that Wright fell through?

Japanese: そッス！　マッタク、用心深い 自分には、考えられないッス。
English: Yup! I can't imagine being that reckless myself...

Japanese: ええと。“石橋をたたいてコワす” ‥‥だったッスっけ？
English: "Look before you cross," is how it goes, right? Or was that "leap"...?

Japanese: それで‥‥ この橋の向こうには、何が‥‥？
English: And? Is there something on the other side?

Japanese: 《奥の院》というボロっちい 建物があるらしいッスが‥‥
English: Yeah, some old building they call the Inner Temple...

Japanese: 橋がないと、向こう側に わたることはできないッス。
English: But we can't get over there without a bridge, sir.

Japanese: なんだと‥‥？
English: What...?

Japanese: ふだんは、ヒトが住んでないから モンダイないッスが‥‥
English: Nobody lives there, so it's usually not a problem...

Japanese: どうやら現在、奥の院には 修行中の人間がいるらしいッス。
English: But someone was at the Inner Temple doing some training and now they're stuck there.

Japanese: 成歩堂から聞いている。 ‥‥真宵くんだろう。
English: Yes, I heard that from Wright. It's Maya Fey.

Japanese: また、あの子ッスか‥‥
English: Oh no... Her again...?

Japanese: とにかく。気流が荒いため、 空からの救助はムズカシイッス。
English: Anyway, the air's really turbulent right now so we can't do an aerial extraction.

Japanese: おそらく、奥の院に行けるように なるのは、明日ッスね。
English: No one's gonna be able to reach the Inner Temple until tomorrow, sir.

Japanese: （‥‥このサムさだ。 ブジならばいいが‥‥）
English: (Will she be alright in this cold...?)

Japanese: そもそも‥‥なぜ、 この橋は燃え落ちたのだろうか。
English: So how did this bridge burn down anyway?

Japanese: それは‥‥おそらく、落雷ッスね。
English: We're almost 100% sure it was lightning.

Japanese: ‥‥らくらい‥‥？
English: ...Lightning?

Japanese: この橋‥‥落雷によって 炎上した、というのか‥‥？
English: You're telling me the bridge caught on fire due to a fluke bolt of lightning?

Japanese: そッス。‥‥ゆうべは、 ３日ぶりに雪が降っていたッス。
English: Yup. Last night it snowed for the first time in 3 days.

Japanese: そんなときに雷が発生するのは、 めずらしいッスが‥‥
English: It's a little unusual for lightning to occur during a snow fall like that...

Japanese: 気象データによると、たしかに カミナリが落ちているッス。
English: But according to the weather data, lightning definitely struck.

Japanese: 事件当夜の《気象データ》を 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Weather Data added to the Court Record.

Japanese: なるほど‥‥。かなり 詳しいデータだが‥‥、
English: Hmm, I see... This is a very detailed weather report...

Japanese: いささか、詳しすぎるな。
English: Almost too detailed...

Japanese: なぜ、橋に落雷した時間まで、 セイカクにわかっているのか‥‥？
English: It even has the exact time that the lightning struck the bridge...

Japanese: あ。それは、目撃証人の証言が あったらしいッス。
English: Oh, that? We got that information from the witness's testimony.

Japanese: 落雷の瞬間を目撃した、だと？ ‥‥いったい、どんな証人だ！
English: Someone actually saw the lightning hit the bridge? Who is this witness?

Japanese: あとで、地元の警察のヒトに 聞いておくッス。
English: Sorry. I'll go ask one of the local cops later, sir.

Japanese: あれ！　なんで御剣検事が それをエリにつけてるッスか？
English: Huh? What's that thing doing on your lapel, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: ‥‥そんなにフシギだろうか。
English: ...Is it really that odd?

Japanese: そりゃそッス！
English: You bet it is, sir!

Japanese: 検事が弁護士バッジを つけてるということは‥‥
English: A prosecutor wearing a defense attorney's badge?

Japanese: 刑事がコロシのライセンスを 持ってるようなものッスからねー。
English: That's like a detective with a license to kill!

Japanese: （そこまで不吉なシロモノに 見えるのか‥‥コイツは）
English: (Does this little thing hold that ominous of a meaning...?)

Japanese: キミなら、これを 見たことがあるだろうか‥‥？
English: Have you ever seen this thing before...?

Japanese: お。ありがたいッス。 ちょうどハラがへってたッス。
English: Hey, thanks a lot. I was getting kind of hungry!

Japanese: ‥‥ぱく。
English: ...*chomp!*

Japanese: なな、ナニをする！　刑事ッ！
English: Wh-What are you doing, Detective!?

Japanese: ぎゃあ！
English: Ack!

Japanese: ‥‥ハラもちのいいキャンデーに 見えちまったッス。
English: ...I thought it was some kind of candy that would fill me up, sir.

Japanese: 最近、ミョーにハラがへって しょうがないッス。
English: Lately I've been feeling so hungry all the time...

Japanese: （むううう‥‥少し給料を 下げすぎてしまったか‥‥？）
English: (Nngh... Maybe his salary has been cut just a little too much...?)

Japanese: どうにも、ヒミツの多い 被害者ッス。
English: There's a lot we don't know about this victim...

Japanese: 本名はおろか、今までの 経歴も、完全に不明ッス。
English: We don't know her real name, her background... We don't know anything really.

Japanese: どうも、妙だな‥‥。
English: That's rather odd...

Japanese: 身分を知られたくないのなら、 なぜ、作家になったのだろうか。
English: If she was trying to hide her identity, why would she become an author?

Japanese: きっと、あれッスね。
English: I'll bet it was just one of those things. You know...

Japanese: こんなに売れっ子になるとは 思わなかったッスよ。
English: She probably never expected to get so popular.

Japanese: ‥‥しょせん自分は、 イッピキの刑事ふぜいッスから。
English: Look, I'm just your everyday, simple detective.

Japanese: あんまりムズカシイことは わからねッス！
English: Don't ask me such tough questions, OK?

Japanese: ‥‥イバるな！
English: ...That's not something to be proud of, Detective!

Japanese: 真宵くんは、ブジなんだろうな。
English: I wonder if Maya is alright?

Japanese: もしものコトがあったら、 成歩堂に合わせるカオがない。
English: I could never show my face to Wright again if something were to happen to her.

Japanese: あー、だいじょぶッス。
English: Aww, it'll be OK.

Japanese: そのときは、自分がナルホドーに カオを合わせとくッスから。
English: If that ever happens, you can just show him my face.

Japanese: ‥‥‥なーんて。
English: How's that...?

Japanese: いやいやいや！
English: Ack! Um, I didn't mean it like that, sir!

Japanese: シャレのめして冗談めかした、 イキでユカイなジョークッス！
English: It was just a silly little joke!

Japanese: なんとか、向こう岸に わたれないものだろうか？
English: I wonder if there is another way to get across to the other side?

Japanese: ‥‥おぼろ橋のワイヤーは、 焼け残っているのだろう？
English: The support wires for the bridge are still intact, correct?

Japanese: 橋は全長２０メートル近く あるッスからねえ‥‥。
English: That bridge is almost 20 yards long...

Japanese: 自分は、見た目ほどの スーパーヒーローではないッス。
English: I know I may look like it, Mr. Edgeworth, but I'm no super hero.

Japanese: （‥‥とにかく、 ブジならばいいが‥‥）
English: (In any case, I just hope Maya is alright...)

Japanese: 私のもとへは、研修中の検事の 情報が入ってくるのだが‥‥
English: I usually hear about promising young prosecutor candidates while they're still in school.

Japanese: こんなオトコ、見たことも 聞いたこともないな。
English: But I've never seen or heard of this person.

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、 ナゾに包まれてるッス。
English: I gotta admit... He's puzzling alright.

Japanese: ハヤテのように現れて、 コーヒー飲んで去ってゆくッス。
English: He just appeared one day, big mug of steaming coffee in his hand...

Japanese: ううむ‥‥気楽なオトコだな‥‥
English: Hmm... He sounds like an interesting man...

Japanese: なんか最近、検事局では コーヒーが大流行中ッス。
English: Yeah... The whole prosecutor's office is really into double espresso macchiatos lately.

Japanese: １年前よりも、引きしまった カオつきになったようだな。
English: Your face is more drawn out than the last time I saw you over a year ago.

Japanese: そ、そッスか？　やっぱり！
English: R-Really? I thought so!

Japanese: いや‥‥
English: No, wait...

Japanese: “引きしまった”というより、 “やつれた”という感じか。
English: It's not so much "drawn out", as "weary and tired", or maybe just plain old "thin".

Japanese: そ、そッスか。‥‥やっぱり。
English: I thought that too, sir.

Japanese: なにぶん自分は、給料が うなだれてるッスから‥‥
English: My salary's been kinda on the low end...

Japanese: どうしても、メシの レパートリーに制限があるッス。
English: So yeah, my food options have been kinda non-existent.

Japanese: 素うどんに素スパゲッティ、 素カレーに素スシに素みそしる‥‥
English: Off-brand chicken soup, off-brand spaghetti, off-brand bread... That sort of stuff.

Japanese: たしか１年前は、ソーメンが 大好物だったはずだが‥‥？
English: I thought you were really into instant noodles last year...?

Japanese: ‥‥さすがに、あきたッス。
English: ...I got really sick of the stuff after a while, sir.

Japanese: 春美くん、か‥‥。 彼女も姿が見えない、とか。
English: What about Pearl...? Has no one seen her at all since last night?

Japanese: そうらしいッスねー。
English: Seems that way...

Japanese: とりあえず、倉院の里には 帰ってないみたいッス。
English: Apparently, she hasn't gone back to Kurain Village either, sir.

Japanese: 事件当夜、天流斎　エリスと いっしょにいた、というのは‥‥？
English: But it's true that she was with Elise Deauxnim at some point last night?

Japanese: まちがいないみたいッス。
English: No doubt about it.

Japanese: ま。まさか‥‥ 絵本作家を殺害したハンニンに‥‥
English: Y-You mean that maybe whoever killed Ms. Deauxnim also...!?

Japanese: ば、バカなコトを言うなッ！
English: D-Don't jump to any crazy conclusions!

Japanese: ぐぎゃああああ‥‥ッス！
English: Oooooouuuch!

Japanese: ‥‥さ、さすが、御剣検事ィィ‥‥ いいパンチを放るッス‥‥！
English: ...You sure have one mean punch, Mr. Edgeworth! It's down-right fierce!

Japanese: ゆうべ、葉桜院の境内で 事件を目撃したッス。
English: This is the witness who saw the crime take place out there in the temple courtyard.

Japanese: かなり、決定的な犯行場面を 見ちゃっているッス！
English: I'm pretty sure it's gonna be her testimony that'll be key to this case, sir.

Japanese: というと‥‥明日の裁判では、 証人として‥‥？
English: So you mean she's going to be a witness in the trial tomorrow...?

Japanese: もちろんッス！
English: Of course!

Japanese: あのオバサンは、信頼できるッス！ もお、勝ったも同然ッスね！
English: A nun is as trustworthy as you can get! We got this one in the bag!
Oh.

Japanese: ‥‥じ、自分は！　自分だけは、 いつも御剣検事のミカタッスから！
English: I-I mean, I've always been on your side, Mr. Edgeworth, sir!

Japanese: いやあ、かなりの ピンチッスよ、これは！
English: So yeah, this is a pretty tight spot we've gotten ourselves into, huh!?

Japanese: （成歩堂が冷や汗まみれに なるのも、わかる気がする‥‥）
English: (Now I understand why Wright is always breaking into a cold sweat...)

Japanese: 彼女には‥‥どこかで 会ったことがあるような気がする。
English: I feel like I've seen this girl somewhere before!

Japanese: 前科‥‥などは、ないのだろうか？
English: She wasn't from a prior case, was she?

Japanese: いや、それはないッス。
English: Nope, can't be.

Japanese: 指紋を照合してみたッスが‥‥ 一致するデータはなかったッス。
English: We did a database search with her fingerprints and came up with nothing.

Japanese: そうか‥‥
English: I see...

Japanese: （‥‥もう少しで 思い出せそうなのだが‥‥）
English: (I just can't shake this nagging feeling...)

Japanese: まあ、御剣検事は プレイボーイッスからねえ。
English: Well, everyone knows that you are quite popular with the ladies, sir...

Japanese: ムカシ、ポイ捨てした ガールフレンドのひとり、とか。
English: Maybe she's an old girlfriend that you sent to Dumpsville when you were younger.

Japanese: けけ、刑事！　いったい、 どこからそんなハナシを？
English: D-Detective! Where did you hear such nonsense from!?

Japanese: いや、なんとなく。 自分のイメージッス。
English: I didn't hear it from anyone. It's just sorta how I imagine you to be... sir.

Japanese: （‥‥わわ、私は そんなふうに見えるのか‥‥？）
English: (...D-Do I really inspire this sort of frothing desire from the female masses?)

Japanese: 天流斎　エリス先生の お弟子さんだそうッス。
English: He's supposed to be Ms. Elise Deauxnim's apprentice or something.

Japanese: ‥‥そうか。
English: ...I see.

Japanese: 似顔絵、描いてもらったッス。
English: Yeah, he drew a nice portrait of me.

Japanese: ‥‥よかった。
English: ...That's nice.

Japanese: でも。５０円、とられたッス。
English: But he made me pay him 50 cents for it.

Japanese: ‥‥キノドクに。
English: ...I'm sorry about that.

Japanese: なんか、気のないそぶりッスね、 御剣検事。
English: You don't seem very interested in him, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥そのオトコのことは、 あまり語りたくない。
English: ...I really don't want to talk about him. That's why.

Japanese: 事件が起こると、かならず ノコノコ出てくるッス。
English: Whenever something happens, this guy always shows up like he hasn't got a care.

Japanese: ‥‥ときどき、自分は こう思うことがあるッス。
English: In fact, you know what...? Every time he shows up, I always wonder the same thing.

Japanese: 『ジツは、すべての事件の 元凶は、コイツかも』‥‥と！
English: "Maybe somehow he's actually the cause of all these incidents!"

Japanese: （この刑事は、気づいて いるのだろうか‥‥）
English: (I wonder if Gumshoe has realized it yet...)

Japanese: （そのコトバ、そのまま キミ自身に当てはまるコトを）
English: (But you could say the exact the same thing about him!)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 葉桜院　本堂
English: February 8 Hazakura Temple Main Hall
*sigh*

Japanese: あ、どうも。 ‥‥どッスか、具合は。
English: Hey! Hello there! Um, so how are you feeling?

Japanese: まあまあ、おかげさまで。 ‥‥あら。こちらは？
English: Alright, I suppose. ...Huh? Who is this?

Japanese: ム‥‥‥。御剣　怜侍と申します。
English: I... My name is... Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: あらあらあらあら。 カワイイ男のコは大カンゲイよぉ。
English: My my my. A handsome boy such as yourself is always welcome!

Japanese: ‥‥ふう‥‥。こんな状況じゃ なかったら、オバサンも‥‥
English: *sigh* If circumstances weren't so tragic, I might just...

Japanese: （お、“男のコ”‥‥） つかぬことを尋ねますが‥‥
English: (Please don't call me "boy".) I'm sorry to trouble you...

Japanese: “ビキニのおばさん” ‥‥を探しているのですが‥‥？
English: But I'm looking for a woman in a bikini.

Japanese: ああ。それなら オバサンのコトね。
English: Well, you have found her. Now, what can I do for you?

Japanese: 見たところ‥‥ ビキニではないようだが。
English: I'm sorry, but I don't see any bikinis...

Japanese: あらあら。オバサンだってねぇ、 スゴいんだから。特に夏は。
English: Ha ha ha! If you ask nicely, I might give you a peek, big boy.

Japanese: わはは。わは。わははははわは
English: Wa ha ha ho ho ho!

Japanese: あの‥‥御剣検事。
English: Umm... Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: このカタ、住職の毘忌尼さん。 ‥‥例の、目撃者ッス。
English: This is the head nun, Sister Bikini... She's the witness.

Japanese: そういうコトは、早く言いたまえ！
English: Why didn't you tell me that earlier!?

Japanese: だからキミの給料は、 いつまでたっても下り坂なのだ！
English: This is exactly why your salary keeps on getting cut!

Japanese: ‥‥ううう。ハラへったッス。
English: Nngh... My stomach is already growling in protest...

Japanese: それで、あの‥‥どうなんですか？ ウチのあやめは‥‥
English: So... Um... What's the latest about my beloved Iris!?

Japanese: とにかく‥‥ お話を聞かせていただきましょう。
English: Well, first... I want to hear what you know.

Japanese: ‥‥ものすごい存在感だ。
English: ...That thing sure casts a strong presence over the whole room.

Japanese: 《小勾玉》と言ってね。 あまたの霊体が宿る宝物だよ。
English: This is the "Lesser Magatama". It's a precious heirloom containing a great many souls.

Japanese: え‥‥！ これが《小》ッスか？
English: Huh...!? This is "lesser"!? What is the "greater" one like?

Japanese: 《大勾玉》は、綾里屋敷の 本堂にまつられているわ。
English: The "Greater Magatama" is displayed in the Main Room of Fey Manor.

Japanese: 先日の《倉院の里・秘宝展》に 出品されるはずだったんだけど‥‥
English: It was supposed to be shown at the Treasures of Kurain exhibit...

Japanese: デカすぎて、会場に 入りきらなかったねぇ。
English: But, it was so large that they couldn't bring it through the doors.

Japanese: 巨大な勾玉をまつる祭壇だ。 キレイに手入れされている。
English: It's an altar with a giant Magatama enshrined on it.

Japanese: ローソクがいっぱい 並んでいるッス。
English: There are lots of candles lined up on it, too.

Japanese: そういえば‥‥
English: You know...

Japanese: バースデーケーキのローソク‥‥ 最近、吹き消してないッス。
English: I haven't had a birthday party for myself in a while...

Japanese: かわりに、そのへんのを 吹き消したらどうか。
English: Maybe you should blow out those candles over there to make up for that.

Japanese: 御剣検事、歌ってくれるッスか？ 《ハッピーバースデー》
English: Mr. Edgeworth? Would you mind singing "Happy Birthday" to me?

Japanese: こ、こらこらこら！ 勝手に消さないでおくれ！
English: S-Stop that! Cut that out! Please don't blow out the altar candles!

Japanese: まがまがしい文字で、 部屋中が埋めつくされている。
English: There's flowing script written everywhere on the walls.

Japanese: こんな部屋で寝たら、 イヤなユメを見そうッスね。
English: Man, I'd bet you'd have awful nightmares if you tried to sleep in here.

Japanese: なにぶん、本堂だからね。 呪詞で守っているわけ。
English: This is our Main Hall, so it's protected by a variety of magic spells and charms...

Japanese: ‥‥邪悪なモノが 入ってこないように。
English: They're all designed to prevent evil forces from entering.

Japanese: お。じゃあ、この部屋で寝たら カゼひかないですむッスね！
English: So if I slept here the uggie- woogie-boogieman wouldn't hunt me down for eternity? Alright!

Japanese: （寝ることしかアタマに ないのか、この男‥‥）
English: (Does this guy think about anything other than eating and sleeping...?)

Japanese: お！　カバチッス！ あったかいカバチッス！
English: Oh! It's a warmed cat box! But where are all the cats?

Japanese: ‥‥カバチではない。 ヒバチだ。
English: ...It's called a "hibachi". It's for heating the room.

Japanese: お！　ザブトンがいっぱい 並んでるッス！
English: Oh! Look at all these ancient straw frisbees!

Japanese: ‥‥ザブトンではない。 円座だ。エンザ、と読む。
English: Those are a type of "zabuton" cushions called "enza"!

Japanese: さっきから、御剣検事、 あげ足とりばっかりッス。
English: Why are you giving me such a hard time? Huh, Mr. Edgeworth? Why?

Japanese: エンザはともかく、 カバチはないだろう！
English: Because learning something new might actually be a good thing for you, Detective!

Japanese: （部屋のすみに、ワラの 円座がつまれている）
English: (There are more enza cushions in the corner of the room.)

Japanese: ‥‥下からのぞいている 白い紙は‥‥なんだろうか。
English: ...What's that white piece of paper sticking out from under that stack...?

Japanese: さあ。なんスかねえ。
English: Hmm... Beats me.

Japanese: ‥‥調べてもらえるかな？ イトノコギリ刑事。
English: Would you mind checking that for me, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: ははは、ハッ！
English: Y-Yes, sir!

Japanese: 御剣検事ィィ！　こんなものがッ。
English: Here you are, Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: みすぼらしい茶封筒だな‥‥
English: It looks like an old manila envelope...

Japanese: んぎゃああああ‥‥ッス！
English: Eeeeeeeeeeaaaah!!

Japanese: ‥‥どうした、刑事？
English: ...What is it, Detective?

Japanese: こ‥‥コイツは‥‥
English: Th-This...

Japanese: 超・重要な手がかりッス！ ある種、スペシャルッス！
English: This could be it! An ultra important clue! A super-special clue!

Japanese: ‥‥とりあえず、 さっさと読みたまえ。
English: ...I suppose I should read it myself then.

Japanese: どうやら、あやめさんに 宛てられた手紙みたいッスね。
English: It looks like a letter addressed to Sister Iris.

Japanese: 『今夜１０時、極楽庵にて待つ。 ‥‥秘密を知られたくなければ』
English: "tonight at 10 at Heavenly Hall. ... ...unless you want your 'secret' to be exposed."

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥脅迫状‥‥？
English: Th-This sounds like a blackmail letter...!

Japanese: 証拠品《あやめ宛てのメモ》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Note to Iris added to the Court Record.

Japanese: それにしても、 さすがは御剣検事ッス！
English: Nice going there, Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: どうしていつも、重要な手がかりを 発見できるッスかねえ‥‥
English: Why can't I ever find clues like that!?

Japanese: 超・検事ッス！ ある種、スペシャルッス！
English: You're an ultra-important prosecutor!! A super-duper prosecutor!

Japanese: ‥‥これを見落とせるほうが ある種、スペシャルだと思うが。
English: Well, I suppose it takes a super-duper kind of dumb to miss a clue like this.

Japanese: まずは‥‥ゆうべのことを お話しいただきたい。
English: First, I'd like to ask you about last night.

Japanese: ゆうべはねぇ‥‥ホラ。 修験者さまがいたでしょ。
English: Well, last night... we had an acolyte here for training.

Japanese: 晩ごはんのあと、奥の院にある 修験堂へ行ったわけよ、ふたりで。
English: After dinner, the two of us went to the Training Hall in the Inner Temple.

Japanese: （真宵くんのことだな‥‥）
English: (She must be talking about Maya...)

Japanese: ‥‥それは、何時ごろの コトだろうか‥‥？
English: Approximately what time was that...?

Japanese: ここを出たのは、９時ごろ‥‥ だったかしらねえ。
English: I suppose it was about 9:00 when we left here.

Japanese: 修行はね。一晩中つづくから。 それはもう、タイヘンなのよお。
English: Training lasts all night long. It's extremely exhausting.

Japanese: 霊行道場の住職は、ずっと つきそっているキマリなの。
English: The channeling dojo's Head Nun must be in attendance at all times to keep watch.

Japanese: いやあ。 それはタイヘンッスねえ！
English: Wow, you're right! That does sound exhausting!

Japanese: ‥‥刑事。 感心してる場合じゃない。
English: Detective, this is no time for flattery.

Japanese: へ。
English: Sorry.

Japanese: あなたは午後１１時ごろ、 境内で、事件を目撃している。
English: Some time around 11:00, you witnessed the incident in the courtyard.

Japanese: 奥の院にいるべきあなたが、 なぜこちらに戻っていたのか‥‥？
English: But your duty was in the Inner Temple. Why did you come back here?

Japanese: ううん‥‥そんなに見つめられたら オバサン、ゾクゾクしちゃうわぁ。
English: Hmm... The way you're staring at me, I'm starting to get goosebumps! Ha ha ha ho ho!

Japanese: （ううう‥‥こっちも 少し、ゾクゾクした）
English: (Nngh... I'm starting to get goosebumps myself, but for a decidedly different reason.)

Japanese: ハッハッ。サムがりッスねー、 御剣検事は。
English: Ho ho, you get the chills pretty easy, don't you Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: 修験者の修行中‥‥住職は、 つきそうギムがあるそうですが？
English: As the head nun, it's your duty to stay with the acolyte at all times, correct?

Japanese: ま、まあまあ、そうなんだけど。
English: Yes, that's correct.

Japanese: オバサンねぇ。こう見えて、 コシが悪いのよぉ。
English: I know I may look strong, but the truth is... I've got a bad lower back.

Japanese: コシ‥‥？
English: A bad lower back...?

Japanese: 冬はね、特にもお、ダメ。 バケツも持てないぐらい。
English: Yes, it's especially bad in the winter. So bad that I can't even lift a bucket.

Japanese: ゆうべは特に冷えこんだでしょ？ コシがバリバリ言いだして。
English: Do you remember how cold it was last night? My bad back felt as stiff as frozen glass.

Japanese: 死ぬ前に、おフロでゆっくり あったまろうと思ったのよぉ。
English: I just wanted to take a nice hot bath to ease my aching back...

Japanese: だから、葉桜院に 戻ってきたワケ。
English: That's why I returned to the Main Hall.

Japanese: ‥‥修験者を、ほったらかして？
English: So you left the disciple all alone?

Japanese: やあねえ！　オバサン、 そんなコトしないわ。
English: Don't be ridiculous! I would never do that!

Japanese: そのために、あやめを呼んだの。 ‥‥消灯の鐘を鳴らしたあとに。
English: That's why I ordered Iris to the Inner Temple after she had rung the bell for lights out.

Japanese: （たしか彼女は‥‥奥の院には 行かなかったはずだが‥‥？）
English: (Yes, but she never went to the Inner Temple, did she?)

Japanese: （この住職は、彼女を‥‥ あやめさんを、見ているのか？）
English: (Did this head nun even see Iris?)

Japanese: （‥‥もう少し、 詳しい情報がほしいな‥‥）
English: (I think I'd better try to get some more details...)

Japanese: それでは‥‥あなたが、境内で 目撃したことを聞かせてください。
English: Alright then... I'd like you to tell me exactly what you saw in the courtyard.

Japanese: あれは、１１時をすぎていたわね。
English: It must have been past 11:00.

Japanese: もう！　もうね！ モロよモロ！　あれは‥‥
English: Ah! No! I can't say it! It-It's too much for my poor heart!

Japanese: ままま。落ちつくッス！ ネクタイ、引っぱっちゃダメッス！
English: Hey! Calm down, lady! L-Let go of my tie!

Japanese: ‥‥ふたつの人影が見えたわ。 ひとりは、もうグッタリしてて‥‥
English: ...I saw two people! One of them was lying on the ground...

Japanese: もうひとりが、うしろから、 か、カタナを‥‥ぷっつりと！
English: Th-The other one was stabbing her from the back... with a sword!

Japanese: その“ハンニン”‥‥ あなたは、目撃されたのですか？
English: Did you see this criminal with your own eyes?

Japanese: 信じたくなかったわ。 あれは‥‥あやめでした！
English: I didn't want to believe what I was seeing...! ...But it was Iris!

Japanese: それは‥‥タイヘンな ショックだったでしょう。
English: You must have been quite shocked.

Japanese: そりゃそッス！ 自分に置きかえて考えてみれば‥‥
English: Of course she was! Try putting yourself in her shoes!

Japanese: 御剣検事、なんと法廷にて 成歩堂弁護士を串刺しィッ！
English: It'd be like if you were stabbing Mr. Wright smack in the middle of a courtroom!

Japanese: イトノコ刑事、なんとそれを 証言台にて目撃ィッ！
English: And I happened to witness it from the witness stand!

Japanese: ‥‥みたいなものッスから。
English: ...I'd be pretty shocked too.

Japanese: あり得ない‥‥でも、 それが現実だったわ。
English: I know it sounds insane... But that's what I saw.

Japanese: 気がついたら、悲鳴を上げていて。 気がついたら‥‥キゼツしていた。
English: And when I finally realized what I was seeing, I screamed, and then... I passed out.

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら‥‥目撃したことに マチガイはないようだな‥‥）
English: (...Unfortunately for us, her testimony seems to be pretty solid.)
However...

Japanese: なんとなく、あのときのあやめは、 不自然だった気がするの。
English: The idea of Iris doing such a foul act seems... unnatural.

Japanese: 不自然‥‥？
English: "Unnatural"...?

Japanese: ‥‥いつもとちがう、 っていうのかしらねェ‥‥
English: The girl I know simply isn't capable of this sort of foulness...

Japanese: （‥‥どういうことだ‥‥？）
English: (...I wonder what she means by that?)

Japanese: その、“修験者さま”と いうのは‥‥
English: So who is this acolyte that was to train at the Inner Temple...?

Japanese: ああ、綾里　真宵さんね。 悪いコトしたワ、あの子には。
English: Her name is Maya Fey... I treated her very badly, I'm ashamed to say.

Japanese: せっかくのスペシャル・コース なのに、ほったらかしに‥‥。
English: And after she went through the trouble of signing up for the Special Course...

Japanese: すぺしゃる・こーす？
English: "Special Course"?

Japanese: 霊氷の上に座って、 呪詞を３万回となえる修行ね。
English: It's a training session where you sit on a block of spirit ice and chant 30,000 times...

Japanese: まさか‥‥ッ！　今もなお 奥の院で、そのスペシャルを‥‥？
English: You don't mean to tell me she's still doing that over at the Inner Temple, do you?

Japanese: いえいえいえいえ！ さすがにそれは、大丈夫よぉ。
English: No, no, no. Of course not! You don't have to worry about that one little bit.

Japanese: ゆうべはまだ、本格的な霊行に入る 前だったからねぇ。
English: Last night, we still hadn't started the training session itself.

Japanese: そ、それならばよいのですが。
English: Well, um... That's good to hear.

Japanese: ‥‥あ！　そうそう。 もう１つ、気になることがあるの。
English: ...Oh! Dear, dear! There's one thing I forgot to tell you.

Japanese: ‥‥聞きたいような、聞きたくない ような。揺れるオトコゴコロッス。
English: ...Uh-oh, I don't think I like the sound of this.

Japanese: 小さな女の子がいたでしょ？ 真宵さんの、妹さんみたいな。
English: Do you know that small girl? I believe she is Mystic Maya's little sister.

Japanese: （真宵くんに、妹がいたのか‥‥）
English: (Maya has a little sister...?)

Japanese: きっと、春美ちゃんッスね。 綾里　真宵のイトコッス。
English: Oh, you mean little Pearl! That's Maya Fey's cousin.

Japanese: はるみ‥‥ちゃん‥‥？
English: Little... Pearl...?

Japanese: ゆうべ、エリス先生といっしょに いたはずなんだけど‥‥
English: I thought she was going to visit Mystic Elise after cleaning up dinner...

Japanese: 今朝から、見てないの！ ‥‥どこにも、いなくて‥‥
English: But I haven't seen her at all since late last night! She's nowhere to be found!

Japanese: な‥‥なんだとッ！ 被害者と、いっしょにいた‥‥！
English: Y-You mean she...!? She was with the victim!?

Japanese: みィんな、オバサンの‥‥ オバサンのコシが悪いのよォォい！
English: It's all the fault of my stupid, creaky old back!

Japanese: （被害者といっしょにいた 女の子が‥‥消えた！）
English: (A little girl who was with the victim on the night of the murder... is gone!)

Japanese: まさに‥‥ 《ナゾはミステリー》ッス！
English: As they say... "The plot thickens!"

Japanese: ダイジなお客さまね、この子は。 エラい修験者さま。
English: She's a very important visitor, you know! An honored acolyte!

Japanese: “エラい”‥‥というのは？
English: "Honored"? How so?

Japanese: 《綾里》といえば、倉院本流の 家元の名字だからねぇ。
English: The Fey name is synonymous with the Kurain Channeling Technique.

Japanese: きっと、チカラのある 霊媒師さんになるハズよぉ。
English: Therefore, she must be a spirit medium of great power indeed.

Japanese: そういえば、１年前‥‥
English: Now that you mention it, one year ago...

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道の《家元》を めぐって、事件が起こったッス！
English: ...there was a case that was about the Master of the Kurain Channeling Technique!
...Detective.

Japanese: 私は、そういう非現実的な話は 好きではないのだよ。
English: I detest talk of supernatural drivel. I suppose now you'll say she has midi-chlorians?

Japanese: ハッハッ。コワがりッスねー、 御剣検事は。
English: Ho ho ho. So it gives you the creeps, huh, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: （‥‥そういうコトでは ないのだが‥‥）
English: (...That's got nothing to do with it.)

Japanese: ああ‥‥この子。 どこへ行ったのかしらねェ‥‥
English: Oh, this poor girl... Where could she have gone...?

Japanese: 調べたところ、おうちにも 帰ってないッス。
English: Well, we checked out her home and she's not there.

Japanese: この葉桜院の近くにも いないのよぉ‥‥。
English: And she's nowhere in the vicinity of the temple either...

Japanese: （そうなると‥‥）
English: (Which means...)

Japanese: （この子がいる場所は、 かぎられてくるな‥‥）
English: (...there's only a few other possibilities as to where she could be.)

Japanese: あ！　吊り橋から落っこちて、 下の川に流された、とか‥‥！
English: Ah! Do you think maybe she fell off the bridge and was carried downstream...!?

Japanese: だからキサマは、なぜ よくない方向にばかり考えるかッ！
English: Why do you have to be such a pessimist, Detective!?

Japanese: うううう‥‥いつもの御剣検事の やり方をマネてみたのに‥‥。
English: Nngh... I was just trying to think like you, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: （キサマとシゴトをすると、 自然とそうなってしまうのだ！）
English: (Ironic. I became a pessimist only after I had the pleasure of working with you!)

Japanese: オバサンのせいなのよねぇ‥‥ おもに、オバサンのコシのせい。
English: It's all my fault... Me and my stupid back!

Japanese: エリスさまが亡くなって、 修験者さまも閉じこめられて‥‥
English: Mystic Elise has been murdered, an important acolyte is trapped...

Japanese: 小さな女の子も愛想をつかして、 どこか行っちゃって。
English: ...and a little girl has been lost!

Japanese: 愛想をつかしたワケじゃ ないと思うッスけど‥‥
English: You know you really shouldn't be so negative. It creates bad karma...

Japanese: ‥‥まだまだ。オバサン、 修行が足りないのねえ‥‥。
English: ...Quite right. I need to do some more training myself, I think...

Japanese: ‥‥うううううッ！
English: ...Ohhhh!

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥こんなコトに なってしまうなんて‥‥。
English: H-How could such a... terrible thing have happened...?

Japanese: この毘忌尼が‥‥毘忌尼のせいで、 とんでもないコトに‥‥ッ！
English: It's all... It's all... It's all my fault!

Japanese: まあまあ。オバサンがセキニンを 感じる必要はないと思うッスけど
English: Well come on, lady... I don't think you need to take all the blame yourself.

Japanese: コラ！　‥‥ボウズに ナニがわかるって言うんだい！
English: Quiet! What do you know anyway!?

Japanese: ‥‥ここ、恐ェッス！
English: ...Ouch. You're scary!

Japanese: ムダにヒゲはやして、 ムダにコート着て‥‥
English: With that stupid 5 o'clock shadow and that stupid old coat of yours...

Japanese: アンタがかわりに やられちゃえばよかったのさ！
English: It's too bad that you weren't the one that disappeared!

Japanese: ‥‥なんでここまで 言われなけりゃならないッス？
English: ...Why does she have to take it all out on me?

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥ すこし、不自然だな‥‥）
English: (Her anger does seem a little... manufactured.)

Japanese: ゆうべ‥‥あなたは、真宵くんと 奥の院・修験堂に行かれたという。
English: You said you went with Maya to the Training Hall in the Inner Temple last night.

Japanese: その際‥‥あやめさんには 会われたのですか？
English: Did you happen to see Iris while you were there?

Japanese: もちろん、会ったわ。
English: Of course I saw her.

Japanese: １０時に《消灯の鐘》を鳴らしたら すぐ来るように、言っといたから。
English: I told her to meet us after ringing the 10:00 bell for lights out.

Japanese: それで、あやめさんは‥‥ 奥の院に来たと言うッスか？
English: So you're saying Iris came to the Inner Temple then?

Japanese: アタリマエでしょお。 スナオな子なんだから、あやめは。
English: Of course she did. Iris has always been a good, obedient girl.

Japanese: それでね。真宵さんの霊行を始めて もらったわけ。あの子に。
English: After that, I had Maya begin her training ceremony.

Japanese: （しかし‥‥あやめさん本人の 供述は、まったくちがう）
English: (But that doesn't fit with Iris's story at all...)

Japanese: （彼女は“奥の院には行ってない” ‥‥と言っている！）
English: (She said that she never went to the Inner Temple!)

Japanese: まさに‥‥ 《ナゾはミステリー》ッスねえ‥‥
English: As they say... "The plot thickens!"

Japanese: 天流斎　エリスさまの お弟子さんでしょ？
English: This is Mystic Elise's apprentice, is it not?

Japanese: ちょっと、オバサンのコト、 気にしてたみたいね。
English: I think he might have a bit of a crush on me... Sweet boy at any rate.
Huh?

Japanese: わかっちゃうのよねェ、オバサン。 ワカい男のコのキモチが。
English: Oh, I don't blame him. Sister Bikini understands the temptations of young men.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: ...Sure.

Japanese: オバサンもアレじゃない。 いちおうホラ、住職だから。
English: But I'm afraid it wouldn't be proper to abuse my position. I am head nun, after all.

Japanese: ま。しかたないから 似顔絵、描かせてあげたワ。
English: But, in return, I did allow him to draw a portrait of me.

Japanese: （描きやすそうなカオだな‥‥）
English: (I pray it was a pose that maintained your modesty. For everyone's sake...)

Japanese: あの。これなんスけど‥‥
English: Umm, about this here...

Japanese: ‥‥そおねえ。 どうかしらねえ‥‥
English: Hmm... Let me see...

Japanese: ホラ。オバサン、アレだから。 おじょーさまだからねェ。
English: Well, as you may know... In order to see reality for what it truly is...

Japanese: 世間知らずのモノ知らず、 恥知らずで親知らずなワケでしょ。
English: ...we strive to break our attachments to much of the transient, material realm.

Japanese: “ハコ入りムスメ”って言うの？
English: I guess you could call me an "immaterial girl"!

Japanese: たしかに、ハコに入りそうな オバサンッスね。御剣検事。
English: I guess she lives in an immaterial world, huh Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥この子、 人生につかれたカオ、してたわ。
English: This man... His face betrays a life of suffering and great weariness of the world.
...Um, sure.

Japanese: でも、まさか‥‥ 川に飛びこんじゃうなんて！
English: But even so... I can't believe this guy actually jumped into the river!

Japanese: 生きてれば、楽しいことも あっただろうに‥‥。
English: Think of all the fun things he might have enjoyed if he had just lived...

Japanese: ナニも、霊行道の修行だけが 人生じゃないのにねえ。
English: Relentless spiritual training alone is no way to lead a complete life, huh...

Japanese: （どうやら、かなりの ゴカイがあるようだが‥‥）
English: (It sounds like she's got some major regrets she's dealing with...)

Japanese: （メンドウだ。‥‥セツメイは 刑事にまかせるとしよう）
English: (Hmm... Perhaps I should let Gumshoe explain Wright's situation to her for me...)

Japanese: じじ、自分ッスか！
English: H-Hey! Don't look at me like that! Do your own dirty work! ...Sir!

Japanese: こ、これ‥‥！
English: Th-That's...!

Japanese: 綾里家に代々つたわる 勾玉じゃないのぉ！
English: That's one of the Fey clan's very own Magatamas!

Japanese: それはそれは、ダイジな 宝玉なのよ、それ。
English: That's a priceless treasure you've got there!

Japanese: さ‥‥さすが、御剣検事ッス！ とにかく、すげェッス！
English: W...Wow! I'm impressed, sir! You never fail to surprise me!

Japanese: それにしても‥‥アンタ、 見かけによらず、マニアねえ。
English: Yes... You don't look it, but now I can see you're a real fan of the occult!

Japanese: はい‥‥？
English: Excuse me?

Japanese: そんなよくできた オモチャ持ち歩いてるんだから。
English: Oh, of course this piece couldn't possibly be real, but to have such a nice replica...

Japanese: 倉院流の大ファンのコね、きっと！
English: Clearly, you are a big fan of the Kurain Tradition!

Japanese: さ‥‥さすが、御剣検事ッス！ とにかく、すげェッス！
English: W...Wow! I'm impressed, sir! You never fail to surprise me!

Japanese: （むやみに見せないほうが よさそうだ‥‥）
English: (I should have known better than to show this thing around...)

Japanese: ‥‥この写真なのだが‥‥
English: About this picture...

Japanese: あらあらあら。オバサン、なかなか よく写ってるじゃなぁい。ねえ？
English: Well look at that! I look pretty s-to-the-exy, don't you think!?

Japanese: そそそ、そッスね。
English: Um, ah... Y-Yeah. Absolutely.

Japanese: いつもはね。こんな雑誌の 取材なんか断っちゃうんだけど。
English: Up until recently, we've avoided exposure in such magazines.

Japanese: 今回は、いろいろ事情が あってねえ‥‥。
English: But this time we had our reasons...

Japanese: “事情”‥‥？ それはいったい、どのような
English: Is that so? For exampl--?

Japanese: まあまあまあまあ。とりあえず、 ビジンに写ってよかったワ。
English: Ha ha ha ha ha!!! Well, why deny the world the sight of such a lovely face!?

Japanese: あ‥‥あらあらあら。 よく見つけたわねぇ！
English: Huh... Ahh! Well, well, well... Look what you've found!

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道の現家元、 綾里　舞子さまよ。
English: That's the Master of the Kurain Channeling Technique, Mystic Misty Fey!

Japanese: アヤサト‥‥‥
舞子さま、姿を消してから、 もう２０年近くたつのよぉ。
English: It's been nearly 20 years since Mystic Misty's disappearance.

Japanese: どうやら、自分のムスメさんに 家元をゆずろうとしてるみたい。
English: Apparently, she intended to pass on the Master title to her daughter.

Japanese: その、ムスメさんというのは？
English: Who is this daughter?

Japanese: ま、まあ。オバサンもいちおう 倉院流の分家なんだけどね。
English: Well, I myself am part of a branch family of the Fey clan.

Japanese: 本家のコトなんて、教えて もらえないのよねぇ‥‥。
English: But even I am not privy to information concerning the main family...

Japanese: まあまあまあまあ！ 《退魔のずきん》じゃない。
English: Well, well, well! That's a Demon-Warding Hood!

Japanese: 修験者さまは、悪霊に ねらわれやすいから。
English: Acolytes are highly susceptible to possession by evil spirits, you know.

Japanese: オバサンたち、いつも これをかぶっているワケよぉ。
English: That's why we always wear these for protection.

Japanese: そうなのですか‥‥。
English: Oh, I see...

Japanese: アンタもホラ。 持ってるだけじゃ、イミないから。
English: What are you waiting for? You won't get any protection just by holding it, you know!

Japanese: ちゃんと、かぶらないと！
English: Put it on already!

Japanese: いや！　私はその。 そういうアレでは‥‥
English: No! I can't...! I was just...!

Japanese: わはは。わははは。わはわははは
English: Ha ha ha ho ho ho!

Japanese: ハッハッハッ！ 御剣検事、才能があるッス！
English: Ho ho ho! It's like it was made just for you, Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: なんの才能だッ！
English: What do you mean by that, Detective!?

Japanese: こりゃケッサクだわ！ しばらく、かぶっといで！
English: It looks absolutely marvelous! You've just got to keep it on for a while!

Japanese: （‥‥何かの罰ゲームか？ これは‥‥）
English: (Is this some sort of divine retribution...?)

Japanese: この、シワくちゃな手紙に 関して、何か知っていますか？
English: Do you know anything about this old, crumpled-up letter?

Japanese: それ‥‥あやめに 宛てられてるのかい？
English: Is that addressed to Iris?

Japanese: ハッキリ《あやめ殿　御中》と 書かれているッス！
English: Yup, it clearly says "To Iris" on it.

Japanese: そんな‥‥あの子にかぎって 《ヒミツ》なんて‥‥
English: I can't believe it... That girl doesn't have any secrets from me...

Japanese: （オバサンは知らなかった ようだな‥‥）
English: (Ah... So Sister Bikini didn't know anything about it...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 葉桜院　境内
English: February 8 Hazakura Temple Courtyard

Japanese: さあ、ここが殺人現場ッス！
English: And this is where the murder took place, sir!

Japanese: 死体以外は、ほぼそのままに 保存されているッス！
English: Other than removing the body, we left everything else untouched!

Japanese: ‥‥ゴクロウ、刑事。 調べさせてもらうとしよう。
English: Thanks, Detective. I'll just have a look around.

Japanese: （まだ、警官たちが 捜査中のようだな‥‥）
English: (It looks like the police are still investigating...)

Japanese: あ‥‥ところで、御剣検事。 念のために、うかがうッスが‥‥
English: Oh yeah, by the way... I thought I'd better ask, just to be sure...

Japanese: 検事は、ホントに弁護するッスか？ あしたの裁判で‥‥あの尼僧を。
English: Are you really gonna defend that nun, Iris, at the trial tomorrow?

Japanese: むう‥‥。引き受けた以上、 全力をつくすつもりだ。
English: Yes, I will... I gave her my word and now I must follow through with my commitments.

Japanese: ‥‥ううううむ。 そうなると、自分の立場上‥‥
English: Well, in that case... I've gotta be careful...

Japanese: ワレワレの捜査状況を、ウッカリ もらすワケには行かないッス。
English: Gotta make sure I don't leak the prosecution's whole investigation...

Japanese: ‥‥よけいなことを シンパイするな、刑事。
English: Don't worry about it, Detective.

Japanese: キミはただ、 ダマって情報をタレ流せばよい。
English: Just keep your mouth closed and I think most of it will flow out on its own.

Japanese: リョーカイッス！　タレ流すッス！
English: Roger, sir! I know exactly what you're saying!

Japanese: う、うム。‥‥その調子だ。 （たまに不安になるな、この刑事）
English: Very well, Detective... (Thankfully his diarrhea of the mouth is permanent...)

Japanese: この黄金像が持っている カタナが‥‥凶器、か。
English: So the sword from this gold statue is actually the murder weapon?

Japanese: そッス。《七支刀》って 呼ばれているらしいッスね。
English: It sure is. It's called a "Shichishito" by the way. Nasty piece of work, sir.

Japanese: 凶器に残っている血は‥‥ 被害者のもの、なのだろうな。
English: There's still blood on it... I suppose this is the victim's blood?

Japanese: そッス。べったりッス。 ‥‥あ。べったりと言えば。
English: Yup. It's all over the blade. And speaking of "all over the blade"...

Japanese: 七支刀のニギリには、 これまたべったりッス。指紋が。
English: There are fingerprints all over the hilt of the Shichishito, too.

Japanese: しもん‥‥？
English: Fingerprints...?

Japanese: モチロン、被告人の尼僧‥‥ あやめのものッス。
English: Naturally they match the prints we got from the younger nun, the defendant.

Japanese: （彼女の指紋が、凶器に‥‥！）
English: (Her fingerprints are on the murder weapon...!?)

Japanese: どうかしたッスか？ ‥‥しんみりして。
English: What's wrong? You're looking really solemn.

Japanese: 成歩堂のヤツ‥‥いつも、こんな 気分で弁護席に立っていたのか。
English: Is this how it is for Wright? Is this what it's like to be a defense lawyer?

Japanese: イヤな感じッスか？　やっぱり。
English: Yeah, I figure it doesn't feel really good.

Japanese: ‥‥ハッキリ言って、 カラダに悪い。
English: ...To be honest, it feels more like it's detrimental to your health.

Japanese: 証拠品《七支刀》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Shichishito added to the Court Record.

Japanese: なんだ‥‥？ この、魔法使いのツエは。
English: What's this...? It looks like a wizard's staff.

Japanese: 被害者の天流斎　エリスが 持っていたものらしいッス。
English: That belonged to the victim, Ms. Elise Deauxnim.

Japanese: アヤシイところは、ないッスね。
English: There's nothing strange or magical about it.

Japanese: ‥‥あ、そうだ！ ここだけのハナシがあるッス。
English: ...Oh yeah! Listen, this is just between us, OK sir?

Japanese: む。‥‥なんだ。
English: Yes... What?

Japanese: これは、ゴクヒッスよ。 ダレにも言っちゃダメッス。
English: This is top-secret stuff! Don't tell anyone about this.

Japanese: ‥‥わかった。
English: ...Alright.

Japanese: じつは‥‥自分はムカシ‥‥ 魔法使いになりたかったッス。
English: The truth is... When I was a kid... I wanted to be a wizard!

Japanese: もしかして‥‥ それで終わりか？
English: That's it? That's what you wanted to tell me?

Japanese: 終わりッス。
English: That's it.

Japanese: （このツエ‥‥がんじょうな 木でできているようだな‥‥）
English: (This staff was made from a very strong kind of wood...)

Japanese: ‥‥指紋は？ 残っていたのか？
English: ...What about fingerprints? Were there any on it?

Japanese: ‥‥付着していたのは 被害者のものだけ、ッス。
English: Just the victim's.

Japanese: 証拠品《被害者のツエ》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Victim's Staff added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 灯ろうだ。‥‥夜になると、 火が入るようだな。
English: It's a lantern... I suppose they light it at night.

Japanese: ほのおの明かりは、ふぜいが あっていいッスよ。
English: There's something elegant about the light of a fire.

Japanese: 自分も、月末は部屋でローソクの ゆらめくほのおを楽しむッス。
English: At the end of each month, I always like to relax in my room by candlelight.

Japanese: ‥‥刑事。 電気代ぐらい払いたまえ。
English: Detective... Can you not afford to pay your electricity bill...?

Japanese: ‥‥なんで わかったッスか‥‥
English: ...How did you know?

Japanese: 葉桜院の本堂が頭上に見える。
English: The Main Hall of Hazakura Temple is above us here.

Japanese: あ、あれ。本堂にはたしか、 ２階はなかったッスよ。
English: Hey... You're right! But I'm pretty sure the Main Hall didn't have a 2nd floor.

Japanese: 葉桜院は、山の急斜面に あるからな。
English: Hazakura Temple was built on a steep part of the mountain.

Japanese: 本堂のオモテ側とウラ側で、 高さがちがうのだろう。
English: The front and back of the Main Hall are on different levels.

Japanese: うううむ。なるほど‥‥
English: Oh... That makes sense...

Japanese: それならいっそ、ナナメに 建てればよかったッスのにね！
English: But wouldn't it be easier just to build the place slanted, sir?

Japanese: （‥‥どうしてその方がよいのか、 まったくリカイできない）
English: (I fail to see how he can consider that to be an even remotely good idea.)

Japanese: 自分は、かなりの スキー好きッス。
English: Ah! I just love skiing!

Japanese: す、“すきーずき”‥‥
English: Really? You don't um... seem like the type.

Japanese: ‥‥ならば、 ソリはどうなのか？
English: Well, what about sleds?

Japanese: いや、自分はスキー好きッスから。 ソリは、ソリが合わないッス。
English: Sleds? Nah. They're a little too kiddy, you know? Messes with my "hard boiled" image.

Japanese: ‥‥どうかしたッスか？ 御剣検事。
English: ...What's with the silence, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: （か‥‥考えすぎ、なのか‥‥？）
English: (I...Is the world starting to go mad?)

Japanese: あの石垣の上は、 山門に通じているはずだ。
English: The Main Gate must be just over that stone wall.

Japanese: ‥‥そッスねー‥‥。 石垣には、いい思い出がないッス。
English: Ah, stone walls... I jumped over a few of those in my time.

Japanese: とはいっても、特にイヤな 思い出があるワケでもないッス。
English: Most of them are good memories, but not all.

Japanese: ‥‥ムリにコメントしなくても いいぞ、刑事。
English: Detective... Perhaps someone should introduce you to the concept of paucity of words...

Japanese: 裁判は明日、だったな。 検事のほうは、どうなっている？
English: The trial begins tomorrow, but who's the prosecutor?

Japanese: この事件の担当は、あの ゴドー検事のはずッスが‥‥
English: I'm pretty sure it's that Godot guy, but...

Japanese: 今、連絡がとれないので 他の検事を探しているらしいッス。
English: ...nobody can get a hold of him, so they're looking for a replacement.

Japanese: どういうことだ‥‥？
English: What do you mean...?

Japanese: いっつもそうッス！　スパッと 連絡がとれたコトがないッス。
English: It's really weird. All of a sudden, no one can reach him!

Japanese: あ。もしかして、あれッスかね！
English: Hmm, I wonder if the rumors are true.

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一がカゼをひいたので、 ヤル気をなくしちまった、とか。
English: Maybe since Mr. Wright caught a cold and won't be defending, he just lost interest.

Japanese: 明日の裁判‥‥私は、特別弁護人 として出廷するつもりだ。
English: I intend to appear in court in the role of defense lawyer.

Japanese: しかし‥‥私が検事だと 知られると、非常にメンドウだ。
English: However... I would be quite unhappy if it came out that I'm actually a prosecutor.

Japanese: そりゃそッスね。 とは言っても‥‥
English: Yeah, I can see why. But I'm not the one you have to worry about...

Japanese: あの裁判長を相手に、 それは、ムリなのでは‥‥？
English: I think the real problem is gonna be that judge...

Japanese: ‥‥さすがに、私のカオぐらいは おぼえているだろうな。
English: ...Yes, he certainly would remember my face, even after such a long absence.

Japanese: だから‥‥ベツの裁判長の 法廷を手配しておいた。
English: That's why I requested another judge preside over the case.

Japanese: 彼とは、一度しか会っていない。 私を忘れている可能性は、高い。
English: We've only met each other once. There's a good chance he won't remember me at all.

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥ 検事のほうは、どうするッスか？
English: Y-Yeah, but... what about the prosecutor?

Japanese: 検事局で、御剣検事を 知らない者はいないッス！
English: Everyone in the prosecutor's office must know you!

Japanese: 事情を知らない検事が 騒ぎ立てたら、ヤッカイッスねー。
English: Wouldn't it be a problem if someone there made a big stink, sir?

Japanese: それならば、問題ない。
English: There's no need to worry.

Japanese: 念のために、検事もすでに、 私のほうで手配しておいた。
English: I pulled a few strings and arranged for a prosecutor of my own choosing.

Japanese: み‥‥御剣検事って‥‥ そんなにエラかったッスかッ！
English: Wow, Mr. Edgeworth... I had no idea you had such a powerful string to pull!

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、刑事。
English: ...Now then, Detective.

Japanese: ゆうべ、ここであったことを、 整理して聞かせてもらいたい。
English: Let's see if we can summarize what we've learned so far.

Japanese: ハッ。‥‥では、見取り図を 見ていただくッス。
English: OK! Let's take a look at the map.

Japanese: ‥‥事件の目撃者・住職の 毘忌尼の証言によると‥‥
English: ...According to the testimony of Sister Bikini, the head nun...

Japanese: 葉桜院で夕食が終わって、 彼女と綾里　真宵は、奥の院へ。
English: ...she and Maya Fey headed to the Inner Temple right after dinner was finished.

Japanese: 午後１０時、あやめが《消灯の鐘》 を鳴らした後、奥の院へ。
English: At 10 PM, after ringing the bell for lights out, Iris went to the Inner Temple.

Japanese: 毘忌尼は、あやめに修行をまかせて 葉桜院に戻っているッス。
English: When she got there, Bikini had her take over while she went back to Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: そして毘忌尼は、葉桜院で おフロに入ったあと‥‥
English: After taking a hot bath to soothe her back...

Japanese: 境内で、殺人の現場を 目撃したッス！
English: ...Sister Bikini witnessed the murder in the courtyard!

Japanese: ま。詳しいコトは、本堂にいる オバサンから聞いてほしいッス。
English: If you want more details, you should ask Bikini herself in the Main Hall.

Japanese: （《奥の院》‥‥か。 もう少し、情報がほしいな‥‥）
English: (The Inner Temple, huh...? I'd like some more information about that place...)

Japanese: 被害者は絵本作家の 天流斎　エリス。本名は不明ッス。
English: The victim is the famous picture book author, Ms. Elise Deauxnim.

Japanese: 去年、絵本の賞をとるまでの 経歴も、まるで不明ッス。
English: Her entire past, up until she won that writing award last year, is a total mystery.

Japanese: ‥‥いまどき、考えにくいな。 《身元不明》‥‥など。
English: It's hard to believe in this day and age you can still find people like that.

Japanese: 被害者の死亡推定時刻は、２月７日 午後１０時から、１１時ごろッス。
English: The estimated time of death of the victim was between 10:00 and 11:00 PM on Feb. 7th.

Japanese: 死因は、背中から刺された 凶器による失血死ッス。
English: Cause of death was blood loss resulting from a stab to the back by the murder weapon.
The murder weapon...?

Japanese: 被害者の身体は、カタナでプッスリ 刺しつらぬかれていたッス。
English: The victim was found skewered with a giant sword, sir.

Japanese: むうう‥‥。キノドクに‥‥
English: Th-That's terrible...

Japanese: それと、もう１点。 奇妙な点があるッス。
English: Yeah, but there's one strange thing.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ。
English: ...Yes?

Japanese: 被害者の全身には、“打ち身”の ようなアトが残っているッス。
English: The victim's entire body was covered with bruises.

Japanese: どうやら被害者は、２階ぐらいの 高さから、落下しているッス。
English: The bruises are consistent with falling from the height of a two-story building.

Japanese: ２階ぐらいの高さ‥‥というと‥‥
English: A two-story building...?

Japanese: ちょうど、あそこに見える 部屋の高さにあたるようだが‥‥？
English: That would be about the same height as that room in front of us, correct?

Japanese: さすが、御剣検事ッス！
English: Hey, you're right. Way to go, Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: 天流斎　エリスはゆうべ、まさに あの部屋に泊まっていたッス。
English: That just happens to be the room that Elise Deauxnim was staying in!

Japanese: （刀で刺しつらぬかれたのち、 突き落とされたのか‥‥？）
English: (Maybe she was pushed out of the window after she was stabbed by the sword?)

Japanese: 証拠品《解剖記録》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Autopsy Report added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 真宵くんが修行をしていた 《奥の院》というのは‥‥？
English: What is this Inner Temple that Maya was supposedly training at...?

Japanese: オバサンの話では、修行をする ためのボロ小屋らしいッス。
English: According to Bikini, it's an old building they use for training the acolytes.

Japanese: おぼろ橋をわたった向こう側に あるッス。
English: It's on the other side of Dusky Bridge.

Japanese: （あの、焼け落ちた橋か‥‥）
English: (The bridge that burned down, huh...)

Japanese: おぼろ橋の向こうは、奥の院の 他に、何があるのだろうか？
English: Is there anything else on the other side of that bridge besides the Inner Temple?

Japanese: 何もないッス。
English: Nope, not a thing.
Nothing?

Japanese: 橋の向こうは、切り立った ガケにかこまれていて‥‥、
English: The other side is surrounded by cliffs on all sides...

Japanese: ある種、“陸の孤島” らしいッス。
English: In a way, it's kinda like a little island out there.

Japanese: （おぼろ橋の向こうに存在するのは 《奥の院》だけ‥‥）
English: (So the only thing there is the Inner Temple...)

Japanese: ヒトの住めるようなトコロじゃ ないらしいッスよ。
English: I hear it's not the kind of place a person could survive in.

Japanese: （ブジでいてくれ‥‥真宵くん）
English: (Please be alright, Maya...)

Japanese: このカタナは、エダわかれ した、人生をあらわしているッス。
English: This sword represents the multiple branches that life can take, all ending as one.

Japanese: ほお‥‥ それは、初耳だな。
English: Hmm... I've never heard that one.

Japanese: ‥‥ときどき、自分は思うッス。
English: You know what I think about sometimes?

Japanese: 殺人課の刑事にならなかったら、 どんな人生があっただろう、と‥‥
English: What kind of life would I have had if I hadn't joined the Homicide Division.

Japanese: ‥‥キミでも、そんなコトを 思うことがあるのか‥‥
English: ...So you think about that kind of thing too, huh?

Japanese: モチロンッス。 いろいろ、想像するッス。
English: Of course I do. I think about it a lot.

Japanese: 交通課の自分。警視総監の自分。 窃盗課の自分。タイホくんの自分。
English: Me as a traffic cop. Me as a detention officer. Me as the Blue Badger...

Japanese: （‥‥警察からはなれる気は ないようだな‥‥）
English: (...I guess he doesn't have any plans to leave the force.)

Japanese: あ、そうそう！　‥‥たぶん、まだ 話したコトがないと思うッスが‥‥
English: Oh, so I think I might have already told you about this, but...

Japanese: じつは‥‥自分はムカシ‥‥
English: The truth is... When I was a little kid...

Japanese: 魔法使いになりたかったのだろう？
English: You wanted to become a wizard, right?

Japanese: えげえええッ！ なんで知ってるッスか！
English: Hey, that's amazing! How did you know that!?

Japanese: すげェッス！　魔法使いッス！ ある種、スペシャルッス！
English: So? What do you think? I'd make a really great one, don't you think?

Japanese: さっき、聞いたばかりではないか！
English: We just had this conversation a little while ago, Detective!
